headline: Scotland Bans Cruel Glue Traps for Trapped Animals
meta_description: Scotland's ban on glue traps has come into full effect, ending the use of sticky adhesive traps that cause severe suffering to animals.
summary_short: Scotland has fully implemented its ban on glue traps, preventing further use of devices that cause severe suffering to trapped animals.
body:
Scotland has taken a firm stand for animal welfare by bringing a complete ban on glue traps into full force. These devices—sticky trays that immobilize small animals—do not kill quickly but instead trap creatures in prolonged suffering. Animals stuck in the adhesive can experience severe distress, injury, and death. The ban represents recognition that this method of pest control inflicts cruelty incompatible with modern values of animal protection.

The legislation signals a shift in how society thinks about our responsibility toward other creatures. Rather than accepting suffering as a necessary cost of convenience, Scotland has chosen a different path. The ban protects not only pets and wildlife that might fall victim to such traps, but also reflects broader commitment to reducing unnecessary harm. As other regions consider similar measures, Scotland's decisive action offers both moral clarity and practical proof that alternatives to cruel methods exist and can be implemented successfully.
